name: CBM (Cubic Metres)
description: Total shipment volume in cubic metres (CBM) from per-carton dimensions in centimetres and the carton count. CBM is the standard volume unit for quoting sea/LCL freight and sizing container loads.

inputs.name: Cartons
inputs.description: Number of identical cartons in the shipment.

outputs.name: Total volume
outputs.description: Total shipment volume in cubic metres.

assumptions: Dimensions are entered in centimetres (1 m3 = 1,000,000 cm3).
All cartons share the same outer dimensions.
Volume is the simple sum of carton volumes; no packing or stacking loss is modelled.
limitations: Uses nominal outer carton dimensions; irregular or bulging cartons occupy more space.
Real-world loadable container volume is below the container's nominal capacity, so CBM alone does not prove a load fits.
Carriers and forwarders may round dimensions or volume when rating.
sources: FedEx 'What is CBM?' shipping glossary page (fedex.com/en-au/shipping/glossary/what-is-cbm.html, checked 2026-07-02): CBM = length x width x height in metres x quantity; the centimetre form here is the same formula with the cm-to-m unit conversion folded into the 1,000,000 divisor.
Standard metric unit conversion: 1 m3 = 100 cm x 100 cm x 100 cm = 1,000,000 cm3.
